name: Ceramic Ink 35ml
description: Highly intensive ink for Ceramic applications.

name: Glossy White
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020°C to 1100°C high coverage white glaze (on light and dark clay bodies); glossy; consistent firing between 1020°C to 1060°C.

name: Glossy Transparent
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020°C to 1100°C best applied in one layer, you can dilute by adding a small amount of water if required.; glossy; consistent firing between 1020°C to 1060°C.

name: Aventurine
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020°C to 1100°C reddish brown with glitter effects; great colouration on dark clays; glossy.

name: Bottle Green
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020° to 1100° glossy; tends to run; consistent firing quality between 1020°-1060°.

name: Spring Green
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020° to 1100° bright apple-green; glossy; can be fired up to 1100 °C.

name: Turquoise Crackle
description: Turquoise, transparent, glossy crackle glaze, must be applied in one coat only since it tends to run strongly glossy; tends to run strongly; consistent firing quality between 1020°C - 1060°C.

name: Oriental Blue
description: Intense and semi transparent blue-turquoise, becomes more greenish as it approaches 1100°C glossy; tends to run; can be fired up to 1100 °C.

name: Transparent Speckled
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020°C to 1100°C transparent with black fine dots; ideal over engobes; glossy.

name: Glossy Gold
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020°C to 1100°C metallic gold colour - must be fired in oxidation for proper colouration; glossy; tends to run strongly.

name: Blue Effect
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020°C to 1100°C greenish blue glaze with light precipitations; produces great depth; glossy.

name: Gloria
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020°C to 1100°C satin glossy; can be fired up to 1100°C; silvery golden at 1020°C.

name: Black
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020°C to 1100°C deep opaque black; apply 2-3 thick coats for full coverage; glossy.

name: Lava
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020°C to 1100°C orange-red with orange peel textured surface; tends to white at edges; apply as a thick coat.

name: Pillar Box Red
description: Liquid earthenware glazes with excellent surface quality, general firing range between 1020°C to 1100°C very strong red; glossy; smooth surface and stable colouring.

name: Engobe White
description: Liquid Stoneware Engobe glazes with excellent matte surface quality, 1180° to 1280°C. Wonderfully stable in firing range 1180-1280°C | cone 4-9; Can be mixed with other Edition colours | 9801 creates pastel shades when mixed; Soft matt/earthy without glaze, good coverage and sharp contours.

description: Liquid Stoneware Engobe glazes with excellent matte surface quality, 1180° to 1280°C. name: PLUS
description: BOTZ Plus increases effects very well on stoneware glazes and enriches the ceramics' brilliance. Apply unevenly on non-fired stoneware glaze, fire at 1250°C / soak 30 min.; Caution: May run!; Apply BOTZ Plus pure on stoneware clay, fired at 1250°C: Result is an awesome glossy blueish crystalline glaze.

name: Transparent
description: striking, transparent glaze with a great glassy surface; very easy to use in a broad firing range from 1020° to 1280°C (cone 05-04 to cone 8-9); apply 1 thick or 2 thin coats.

name: Opal White
description: striking, semi-transparent glaze with a great glassy surface; strong shining and pastel colours; very easy to use in a broad firing range from 1020° to 1280°C (cone 05-04 to cone 8-9) • at 1050°C: very colourful and almost opaque / apply 2-3 times.

name: Egg Yellow
description: Versatile ceramic decoration colours for underglaze, on-glaze, or in-glaze design. Suitable for firing temperatures between 1000–1250°C (cone 06–6). The BOTZ Unidekor range offers incredible creative freedom for ceramic artists. Whether you're decorating bisque, working with raw glaze, or applying design after glazing, these colours adapt beautifully across multiple techniques. With rich pigmentation, smooth application, and consistent results, Unidekor is ideal for both functional ware and artistic surface decoration. Universal ceramic colour system — use as underglaze, on-glaze, or in-glaze; Firing stable from 1000–1250°C with satin-gloss finish; Highly versatile application methods: • Underglaze: apply to bisque and overglaze with BOTZ 9106 (recommended).
